---
title: "Foreign Policy, Defence & Geopolitics"
description: "State behaviour, deterrence, world orders, and India's strategic choices."
listing:
  contents: "*.qmd"
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 2
  fields: [image, title, summary]
  field-display-names:
    title: "Framework"
    summary: "What it says"
  image-height: "180px"
  sort:
    - "title asc"
  filter-ui: true
  sort-ui: false
  page-size: 50
---

Frameworks in this section concern how states behave towards each other — how they describe their interests, deter rivals, and position themselves in a changing world order.

If you are new here, start with **Describing State Policy on Geopolitical Issues** (a taxonomy for talking clearly about what any government actually does in foreign policy) and **Guns and Butter** (the fundamental trade-off between security and prosperity that shapes most strategic choices). India-specific cases run throughout these frameworks — they are written from a perspective that takes India's strategic constraints seriously.
